# simple-liquid-glass

> Zero-dependency React "liquid glass" component. It renders Apple-style liquid glass with an SVG
> displacement map (real refraction on Chromium), and ships a framework-agnostic `<liquid-glass>`
> web component for Vue/Svelte/Astro/vanilla. React 16.8–19, SSR-safe. ~10 KB React core /
> ~6.5 KB web component, gzip.
>
> **Support rule:** refraction is Chromium-only; Safari/iOS/Firefox render frosted glass (no
> refraction); there is no `backdropRef`/`mirror`.

This file is written for LLMs/agents: it contains the complete public API, copy-paste recipes, and
the constraints needed to use the library correctly and autonomously. When in doubt, prefer the
patterns here over guessing. Version: 3.x.

## Install

```bash
npm install simple-liquid-glass
```

Peer deps: `react` and `react-dom` (16.8 – 19). The web component needs no React.

## Entry points (exports)

- `simple-liquid-glass` → `LiquidGlass` (default and named export). Types: `LiquidGlassProps`,
  `LiquidGlassHandle`, `LiquidPreset` (`'ripple' | 'flow' | 'wobble'`), `LensMode`
  (`'classic' | 'convex' | 'shift' | 'rim'`).
- `simple-liquid-glass/interactive` → `LiquidGlassInteractive`, `usePointerElastic(ref, options)`.
  Types: `LiquidGlassInteractiveProps`, `PointerElasticOptions`, `RippleEffect` (`'ripple' | 'drop'`),
  `LiquidTrigger` (`'always' | 'hover' | 'press'`).
- `simple-liquid-glass/web-component` → side-effect import; registers the `<liquid-glass>` custom
  element. Also exports `defineLiquidGlass(tag?)` and `LiquidGlassElement`.

## How refraction works — and the #1 thing to get right

The real refraction is an SVG displacement map driven through `feDisplacementMap` inside
`backdrop-filter`. **That only runs on Chromium** (Chrome/Edge/Android Chrome). On Safari, iOS, and
Firefox, `backdrop-filter` cannot run SVG filters, so the component renders a deliberate, polished
**frosted glass** (blur + saturation + subtle tint + gradient border) — real glass, just not
refraction. This is a hard platform limitation: WebKit can't run SVG filters in `backdrop-filter`,
and no web API exposes a live page backdrop to WebGL/canvas. There is no `backdropRef`/`mirror` and
no workaround that delivers live refraction on those engines.

| Engine | Refraction |
|---|---|
| Chrome / Edge / Android Chrome (Chromium) | ✅ real SVG refraction |
| Safari / iOS / Firefox | frosted glass (no refraction) |

## `<LiquidGlass>` props (React core)

| prop | type | default | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `children` | ReactNode | — | content inside the glass |
| `mode` | `'preset' \| 'custom'` | `'preset'` | preset applies tuned defaults; props still override |
| `scale` | number | `160` | displacement strength (negative inverts the bend) |
| `radius` | number | `50` | border radius (px) |
| `border` | number | `0.05` | border thickness 0–0.5 |
| `lightness` | number | `53` | glass lightness 0–100 |
| `displace` | number | `5` | displacement-map blur amount |
| `alpha` | number | `0.9` | glass alpha 0–1 |
| `blur` | number | `0` | post blur |
| `dispersion` | number | `50` | chromatic dispersion |
| `saturation` | number | `140` | CSS saturate() % |
| `aberrationIntensity` | number | `0` | chromatic aberration multiplier |
| `frost` | number | `0.1` | frost intensity 0–1 |
| `borderColor` | string | `rgba(120,120,120,0.7)` | gradient border color |
| `glassColor` | string | `rgba(255,255,255,0.4)` | must be semi-transparent |
| `background` | string | — | solid/gradient; auto made semi-transparent |
| `autoTextColor` | boolean | `false` | auto pick text color from background luminance |
| `textOnDark` / `textOnLight` | string | `#ffffff` / `#111111` | text colors for autoTextColor |
| `forceTextColor` | boolean | `false` | force computed text color on descendants |
| `angle` | number | `0` | **3.0** refraction direction in degrees; positive = clockwise; shape-adapted so it's faithful on any aspect. Chromium SVG path. |
| `shapeAdapt` | boolean | `true` | **3.0** aspect-faithful + isotropic refraction (glass cut to the element's shape). `false` = legacy ≤2.x map. |
| `lens` | `'classic' \| 'convex' \| 'shift' \| 'rim'` | `'classic'` | **3.0** displacement field: classic radial / dome magnifier / uniform offset / clear-center rim |
| `lensStrength` | number | `1` | **3.0** lens magnitude (0 = off) |
| `lensCenter` | `[number, number]` | `[0.5, 0.5]` | **3.0** lens center (0–1) for convex/rim |
| `liquid` | `'ripple' \| 'flow' \| 'wobble' \| false` | `false` | **3.0** real animated refraction (Chromium; in-view + reduced-motion gated) |
| `liquidSpeed` | number | `1` | **3.0** animation rate |
| `liquidScale` | number | preset | **3.0** distortion amplitude (px) |
| `quality` | `'low' \| 'standard' \| 'high' \| 'extreme'` | `'low'` | map resolution / pass count. `low` = clean single-pass; `standard`+ add 3-pass chromatic aberration |
| `autodetectquality` | boolean | `false` | pick quality from device performance |
| `mobileFallback` | `'css-only' \| 'svg'` | css-only on mobile | mobile render strategy |
| `effectMode` | `'auto' \| 'svg' \| 'blur' \| 'off'` | `'auto'` | force a strategy |
| `className` / `style` | — | — | standard; plus any HTML div attributes |

Ref (`LiquidGlassHandle`): `{ element: HTMLDivElement | null; getQuality(): quality }`.

## `<LiquidGlassInteractive>` — extra props (import from `simple-liquid-glass/interactive`)

Extends all `LiquidGlassProps` and adds pointer reactivity:

| prop | type | default | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `elasticity` | number | `0.15` | how far the glass leans toward the cursor (0 = rigid) |
| `maxShift` | number | `18` | max lean translate (px) |
| `stiffness` / `damping` | number | `0.18` / `0.78` | spring tuning |
| `specular` | boolean | `false` | pointer-tracked specular highlight (opt-in) |
| `followPointer` | boolean | `false` | a refractive bump that distorts the backdrop toward the cursor |
| `clickRipple` | `boolean \| 'ripple' \| 'drop'` | `false` | real water-like ripple on click (`true` = `'ripple'`; `'drop'` = droplet-in-water rings). Lag-free, compositor-driven, reduced-motion-aware |
| `rippleIntensity` | number | `1` | ripple brightness/reach |
| `liquidTrigger` | `'always' \| 'hover' \| 'press'` | `'always'` | when `liquid` animates (hover/press stay idle/zero-cost until interaction) |

Also: `usePointerElastic(ref, options)` — apply the elastic spring to any `LiquidGlassHandle` ref.

## `<liquid-glass>` web-component attributes (kebab-case, all optional)

`radius`, `frost`, `blur`, `saturation`, `displace`, `scale`, `border-color`, `lightness`, `alpha`,
`angle`, `shape-adapt` (`"false"` to disable), `lens`, `lens-strength`, `lens-center` (`"x,y"`),
`liquid`, `liquid-speed`, `liquid-scale`.

Like the React core, the web component renders real SVG refraction on Chromium and a frosted-glass
fallback on Safari/iOS/Firefox. There is no `backdrop-selector`/`backdrop`/`mirror`/`mirror-scale`/
`track` — and no refraction on the non-Chromium engines.

Note: pointer interaction props (`followPointer`, `clickRipple`, `rippleIntensity`, `liquidTrigger`,
elasticity) are **React-only** (`LiquidGlassInteractive`) — they are NOT web-component attributes.
Give the element an explicit size and place it over a background.

## Recipes

### React — basic
```tsx
import { LiquidGlass } from 'simple-liquid-glass'

<LiquidGlass radius={24} style={{ width: 320, height: 180 }}>
  <div style={{ padding: 20 }}>Frosted, refractive glass</div>
</LiquidGlass>
```

### React — lens modes + directional angle (3.0)
```tsx
<LiquidGlass lens="convex" lensStrength={1.2} style={{ width: 200, height: 200 }} />
<LiquidGlass lens="shift" angle={30} style={{ width: 420, height: 64 }} /> {/* navbar */}
<LiquidGlass lens="rim" style={{ width: 160, height: 160 }} />
```

### React — real animated "liquid" refraction (3.0)
```tsx
<LiquidGlass liquid="ripple" liquidSpeed={1} style={{ width: 320, height: 200 }}>
  {/* backdrop genuinely warps; pauses off-screen + on prefers-reduced-motion; Chromium */}
</LiquidGlass>
```

### React — interactive: follow pointer + click ripples (3.0)
```tsx
import { LiquidGlassInteractive } from 'simple-liquid-glass/interactive'

<LiquidGlassInteractive elasticity={0.3} followPointer clickRipple="drop">
  Move me, then click for a water-drop ripple
</LiquidGlassInteractive>
```

### Vue / Svelte / Astro / plain HTML — web component
```html
<script type="module"
  src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/simple-liquid-glass/web-component"></script>

<liquid-glass radius="20" frost="0.15" lens="convex" angle="20"
  style="width:320px;height:200px;display:block">
  Glass anywhere
</liquid-glass>
```

### Next.js / SSR
SSR-safe out of the box — it only touches the DOM in effects. Use it in client components; no
`'use client'` gymnastics needed beyond your normal component boundaries.

## Constraints & gotchas (read before generating code)

- **Refraction (SVG displacement) is Chromium-only.** Chrome/Edge/Android Chrome get real refraction;
  Safari/iOS/Firefox render frosted glass (blur + saturation + subtle tint + gradient border). There
  is no `backdropRef`/`mirror` and no way to get live refraction on the non-Chromium engines.
- **`liquid` and `followPointer`/`clickRipple` are GPU-real and gated**: animate only while on-screen,
  pause on `prefers-reduced-motion`, Chromium SVG path; treat `liquid` as a per-element design choice,
  not for hundreds of cards. `liquidTrigger: 'hover' | 'press'` costs nothing until interaction.
- **`shapeAdapt` defaults to `true` in 3.0** and changes how non-square lenses look at `angle: 0`
  versus 2.x (square lenses are unchanged). Set `shapeAdapt={false}` for the exact legacy map.
- **`glassColor` must be semi-transparent** (rgba/hsla/hex-with-alpha); opaque values fall back to frost.
- Give the element an explicit size; the effect fills 100% of its box.
- Many glass instances? They auto-drop their `backdrop-filter` when off-screen (IntersectionObserver).
- `clickRipple` / `rippleIntensity` / `followPointer` / `liquidTrigger` are on `LiquidGlassInteractive`
  only (the `/interactive` import), not the base component or the web component.

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/simple-liquid-glass
- repo: https://github.com/lucaperullo/simple-liquid-glass
- changelog: https://github.com/lucaperullo/simple-liquid-glass/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
